The Pitmaster

Craft Runs Deep.
Kenyon Railey.

For Kenyon Railey, barbecue was never a hobby — it was a calling. As founder and pitmaster of H&B Barbecue, Kenyon has spent years perfecting every detail: the depth of the bark, the clarity of the smoke ring, the exact moment a brisket transitions from good to extraordinary.

Every morning before dawn, he loads fresh Post Oak into the offset smokers himself. By the time the doors open at 11, each cut has absorbed over fourteen hours of clean, steady smoke at 225°F. No shortcuts. No gas-assist. No holding ovens. Just salt, pepper, oak, and an obsessive commitment to the Texas tradition.

When it's gone, it's gone. That's the standard Kenyon set from day one — and it's the standard that keeps Dallas coming back.
